answer:
An Airtable MCP server is an integration that allows AI models to interact directly with your Airtable bases using the Model Context Protocol [1][2]. These servers enable LLMs to perform operations such as listing bases and tables, managing schemas, and performing CRUD operations on records [1][3][4].

To use one, you typically install it via npm and configure your MCP client (such as Claude Desktop or Cursor) with your Airtable Personal Access Token (PAT) as an environment variable [2][5]. Multiple implementations exist with varying features, ranging from standard record management to advanced tools like AI-powered analytics, batch processing, and governance controls [1][3][6][7].
